assumptions: the first ZILFIT release should use one hero product
customer-facing UX should remain separate from technical agent logic
claim wording must be approved before public rendering
fit summary packets should be reusable across recommendation and feedback loops
risks: unsafe wording may leak into user-facing copy
weak scan data may be routed too early
technical packets may be confused with customer-facing outputs
feedback may be overinterpreted without structured routing
next_required_validation: Hermes review before app integration and multi-agent routing activation
primary_user_goal: Help a first-time user understand the product quickly, complete a guided foot scan, receive a simple recommendation, and finish checkout with minimal friction.
ux_recommendation: Keep Z-UX as the user-facing orchestration layer, while routing wording, simulation, CAD, patent, and research logic through the correct governed downstream agents.
next_design_step: Turn this handoff map into implementation-ready event contracts and packet schemas for the first app build.
